title: Google i18n-address dataset
description: Schema for the flattened i18n-address.json payload containing data/* and examples/* records.

countries.description: Identifies the countries for which data is provided.

id.description: The unique ID of the region, in the form of a path from parent IDs to the key.

key.description: The key of the region, unique to its parent. If there is an accepted abbreviation for this region, then the key will be set to this and name will be set to the local name for this region. If there is no accepted abbreviation, then this key will be the local name and there will be no local name specified. This value must be present.

sub_keys.description: Encodes the KEY value of all the children of this region.

lname.description: The latin name of this region, if the local name is not in latin script already.

lfname.description: The full latin name of this region, if the local name is not in latin script already.

sub_names.description: Encodes the local name value of all the children of this region.

sub_lnames.description: Encodes the transliterated latin name value of all the children of this region, if the local names are not in latin script already.

sub_lfnames.description: Encodes the full latin name value of all the children of this region, if the local names are not in latin script already.

fmt.description: The standard format string. This identifies which fields can be used in the address, along with their order. This also carries additional information for use in formatting the fields into multiple lines. This is also used to indicate which fields should _NOT_ be used for an address.

lfmt.description: The latin format string FMT used when a country defines an alternative format for use with the latin script, such as in China.

isoid.description: The CLDR (Common Locale Data Repository) country code (https://cldr.unicode.org) for this region, if there is one. This value must be present.

sub_isoids.description: Encodes the CLDR country code (https://cldr.unicode.org) for all the children of this region, if there is one.

lang.description: The default language of any data for this region, if known.

languages.description: The languages used by any data for this region, if known.

require.description: Indicates which fields must be present in a valid address.

xrequire.description: Indicates which fields must be present in a valid address for the subtree beneath this region.

sub_xrequires.description: Indicates which fields must be present in a valid address for all the children of this region.

sub_mores.description: Indicates, for each child of this region, whether that child has additional children.

upper.description: Indicates which fields should be uppercased when formatting an address.

zip.description: Encodes the postal code pattern if at the country level, and the postal code prefix if at a level below country.

xzip.description: Encodes the ZIP value for the subtree beneath this region.

zipex.description: Examples of postal codes for the subtree beneath this region. Use comma to separate multiple examples.

sub_zips.description: Encodes the postal code pattern for all the children of this region.

sub_xzips.description: Encodes the ZIP value for all the children of this region.

sub_zipexs.description: Examples of postal codes for all the children of this region. Use comma to separate multiple examples.

state_name_type.description: Indicates the type of the name used for the state (administrative area) field.

zip_name_type.description: Indicates the type of the name used for the ZIP (postal code) field.

locality_name_type.description: Indicates the type of the name used for the locality (city) field.

sublocality_name_type.description: Indicates the type of the name used for the sublocality field.

types.description: Available example types (for example local, latin), separated by '~'.
